text: I'll start by reading the relevant files to understand the existing patterns.

content:
1	package v1alpha1
2	
3	import (
4		apiv1 "github.com/openshift/api/operator/v1"
5		corev1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
6		networkingv1 "k8s.io/api/networking/v1"
7		metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
8	)
9	
10	// CertManagerSpec defines the desired state of CertManager.
11	type CertManagerSpec struct {
12		apiv1.OperatorSpec `json:",inline"`
13	
14		// ControllerConfig specifies further customization options for the controller's deployment spec.
15		//
16		// Possible customizations include the following,
17		//
18		// For OverrideArgs,
19		// This field appends values to .spec.template.spec.containers[...].args. The container
20		// that will be selected will be based on image present on the spec. Possible values are,
21		// Eg:
22		//   - "--acme-http01-solver-nameservers="8.8.8.8:53,1.1.1.1:53"
23		//   - "--dns01-recursive-nameservers=8.8.8.8:53,1.1.1.1:53"
24		//   - "--dns01-recursive-nameservers-only"
25		//
26		// For OverrideEnvs,
27		// This field appends values to .spec.template.spec.containers[...].env. The container
28		// that will be selected will be based on image present on the spec. Possible values are,
29		// Eg:
30		//   - "HTTP_PROXY": "http://proxy:8080"
31		//
32		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
33		// +optional
34		ControllerConfig *DeploymentConfig `json:"controllerConfig,omitempty"`
35	
36		// WebhookConfig specifies further customization options for the webhook's deployment spec.
37		//
38		// Possible customizations include the following,
39		//
40		// For OverrideArgs,
41		// --config string                                Path to a file containing a WebhookConfiguration object used to configure the webhook
42		// --dynamic-serving-ca-secret-name string        name of the secret used to store the CA that signs serving certificates
43		// --dynamic-serving-ca-secret-namespace string   namespace of the secret used to store the CA that signs serving certificates
44		// --dynamic-serving-dns-names strings            DNS names that should be present on certificates generated by the dynamic serving CA
45		// --v Level                                      number for the log level verbosity
46		//
47		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
48		// +optional
49		WebhookConfig *DeploymentConfig `json:"webhookConfig,omitempty"`
50	
51		// CAInjectorConfig specifies further customization options for the cainjector's deployment spec.
52		//
53		// Possible customizations include the following,
54		//
55		// For OverrideArgs,
56		// --namespace string                          If set, this limits the scope of cainjector to a single namespace. If set, cainjector
57		//                                             will not update resources with certificates outside of the configured namespace.
58		// --v Level                                   number for the log level verbosity
59		//
60		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
61		// +optional
62		CAInjectorConfig *DeploymentConfig `json:"cainjectorConfig,omitempty"`
63	
64		// DefaultNetworkPolicy enables the default network policy for cert-manager components.
65		// When set to "true", the operator will create default network policies to secure
66		// communication between cert-manager controller, webhook, and cainjector components.
67		// When set to "false" or empty, no default network policies are created.
68		// Valid values are: "true", "false", or empty (default: false).
69		//
70		// This field is immutable once set to "true" for security reasons. Network policies
71		// cannot be disabled once enabled to prevent accidental security degradation.
72		// Users should carefully plan their network policy requirements before enabling this field.
73		//
74		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
75		// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:="true";"false";""
76		// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="oldSelf != 'true' || self == 'true'",message="defaultNetworkPolicy cannot be changed from 'true' to 'false' once set"
77		// +optional
78		DefaultNetworkPolicy string `json:"defaultNetworkPolicy,omitempty"`
79	
80		// NetworkPolicies specifies the egress network policy configuration to be applied to cert-manager
81		// pods/operands when DefaultNetworkPolicy is "true". By default, enabling network policies
82		// creates a deny-all policy that blocks all outgoing traffic from cert-manager components.
83		// Ingress rules are automatically handled by the operator based on the current running ports.
84		// Use this field to provide the necessary egress policy rules that allow required outbound traffic
85		// for cert-manager to function properly (e.g., API server communication, external issuer access, etc.).
86		//
87		// Each NetworkPolicy in this slice will be created as a separate Kubernetes NetworkPolicy
88		// resource. Multiple policies can be defined to organize egress rules logically (e.g., separate
89		// policies for different types of outbound traffic or different security zones).
90		//
91		// This field is only effective when DefaultNetworkPolicy is set to "true".
92		// If DefaultNetworkPolicy is "true" but this field is not provided, cert-manager
93		// components will be isolated with deny-all egress policies.
94		//
95		// This field is immutable once DefaultNetworkPolicy is set to "true" for security reasons.
96		//
97		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
98		// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="oldSelf.all(op, self.exists(p, p.name == op.name && p.componentName == op.componentName))",message="name and componentName fields in networkPolicies are immutable"
99		// +kubebuilder:validation:MinItems:=0
100		// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxItems:=50
101		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
102		// +listType=map
103		// +listMapKey=name
104		// +listMapKey=componentName
105		// +optional
106		NetworkPolicies []NetworkPolicy `json:"networkPolicies,omitempty"`
107	}
108	
109	// DeploymentConfig defines the schema for
110	// overriding deployment of cert-manager operands,
111	// namely the controller, webhook and cainjector.
112	type DeploymentConfig struct {
113	
114		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
115		// +optional
116		OverrideArgs []string `json:"overrideArgs,omitempty"`
117	
118		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
119		// +optional
120		OverrideEnv []corev1.EnvVar `json:"overrideEnv,omitempty"`
121	
122		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
123		// +optional
124		OverrideLabels map[string]string `json:"overrideLabels,omitempty"`
125	
126		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
127		// +optional
128		OverrideResources CertManagerResourceRequirements `json:"overrideResources,omitempty"`
129	
130		// OverrideReplicas defines the number of replicas for the operand deployment.
131		// If not specified, the default replicas from the deployment manifest will be used.
132		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
133		// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1
134		// +optional
135		OverrideReplicas *int32 `json:"overrideReplicas,omitempty"`
136	
137		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
138		// +optional
139		OverrideScheduling CertManagerScheduling `json:"overrideScheduling,omitempty"`
140	}
141	
142	// CertManagerResourceRequirements describes the compute resource requirements for the cert-manager operands,
143	// namely the controller, webhook and cainjector.
144	type CertManagerResourceRequirements struct {
145		// Limits describes the maximum amount of compute resources allowed.
146		// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/
147		// +optional
148		Limits corev1.ResourceList `json:"limits,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,1,rep,name=limits,casttype=ResourceList,castkey=ResourceName"`
149		// Requests describes the minimum amount of compute resources required.
150		// If Requests is omitted for a container, it defaults to Limits if that is explicitly specified,
151		// otherwise to an implementation-defined value.
152		// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/
153		// +optional
154		Requests corev1.ResourceList `json:"requests,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,2,rep,name=requests,casttype=ResourceList,castkey=ResourceName"`
155	}
156	
157	// CertManagerScheduling describes the scheduling configurations for the cert-manager operands,
158	// namely the controller, webhook and cainjector.
159	type CertManagerScheduling struct {
160		// NodeSelector is a selector which must be true for the pod to fit on a node.
161		// Selector which must match a node's labels for the pod to be scheduled on that node.
162		// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/
163		// +optional
164		// +mapType=atomic
165		NodeSelector map[string]string `json:"nodeSelector,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,7,rep,name=nodeSelector"`
166	
167		// Tolerations are attached to the pod to tolerate any taint that matches the
168		// taint's key, value and effect using the toleration's matching operator.
169		// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/taint-and-toleration/
170		// +optional
171		// +listType=atomic
172		Tolerations []corev1.Toleration `json:"tolerations,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,22,opt,name=tolerations"`
173	}
174	
175	// CertManagerStatus defines the observed state of CertManager.
176	type CertManagerStatus struct {
177		apiv1.OperatorStatus `json:",inline"`
178	}
179	
180	// +genclient
181	// +genclient:nonNamespaced
182	// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object
183	//+kubebuilder:object:root=true
184	//+kubebuilder:subresource:status
185	//+kubebuilder:resource:scope=Cluster
186	
187	// CertManager is the Schema for the certmanagers API
188	// +operator-sdk:csv:customresourcedefinitions:displayName="CertManager"
189	type CertManager struct {
190		metav1.TypeMeta   `json:",inline"`
191		metav1.ObjectMeta `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
192	
193		// +kubebuilder:validation:Required
194		// +required
195		Spec CertManagerSpec `json:"spec,omitempty"`
196		// +optional
197		Status CertManagerStatus `json:"status,omitempty"`
198	}
199	
200	type UnsupportedConfigOverrides struct {
201		Controller UnsupportedConfigOverridesForCertManagerController `json:"controller,omitempty"`
202		Webhook    UnsupportedConfigOverridesForCertManagerWebhook    `json:"webhook,omitempty"`
203		CAInjector UnsupportedConfigOverridesForCertManagerCAInjector `json:"cainjector,omitempty"`
204	}
205	
206	type UnsupportedConfigOverridesForCertManagerController struct {
207		Args []string `json:"args,omitempty"`
208	}
209	
210	type UnsupportedConfigOverridesForCertManagerWebhook struct {
211		Args []string `json:"args,omitempty"`
212	}
213	
214	type UnsupportedConfigOverridesForCertManagerCAInjector struct {
215		Args []string `json:"args,omitempty"`
216	}
217	
218	// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object
219	//+kubebuilder:object:root=true
220	
221	// CertManagerList contains a list of CertManager.
222	type CertManagerList struct {
223		metav1.TypeMeta `json:",inline"`
224		metav1.ListMeta `json:"metadata"`
225	
226		Items []CertManager `json:"items"`
227	}
228	
229	// ComponentName represents the different cert-manager components that can have network policies applied.
230	type ComponentName string
231	
232	const (
233		// CAInjector represents the cert-manager CA injector component.
234		CAInjector ComponentName = "CAInjector"
235	
236		// CoreController represents the cert-manager core controller component.
237		CoreController ComponentName = "CoreController"
238	
239		// Webhook represents the cert-manager webhook component.
240		Webhook ComponentName = "Webhook"
241	)
242	
243	// NetworkPolicy represents a custom network policy configuration for operator-managed components.
244	// It includes a name for identification and the network policy rules to be enforced.
245	type NetworkPolicy struct {
246		// Name is a unique identifier for this network policy configuration.
247		// This name will be used as part of the generated NetworkPolicy resource name.
248		// +kubebuilder:validation:MinLength:=1
249		// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxLength:=253
250		// +kubebuilder:validation:Required
251		// +required
252		Name string `json:"name"`
253	
254		// ComponentName represents the different cert-manager components that can have network policies applied.
255		// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=CoreController
256		// +kubebuilder:validation:Required
257		// +required
258		ComponentName ComponentName `json:"componentName"`
259	
260		// egress is a list of egress rules to be applied to the selected pods. Outgoing traffic
261		// is allowed if there are no NetworkPolicies selecting the pod (and cluster policy
262		// otherwise allows the traffic), OR if the traffic matches at least one egress rule
263		// across all of the NetworkPolicy objects whose podSelector matches the pod. If
264		// this field is empty then this NetworkPolicy limits all outgoing traffic (and serves
265		// solely to ensure that the pods it selects are isolated by default).
266		// The operator will automatically handle ingress rules based on the current running ports.
267		// +optional
268		// +listType=atomic
269		Egress []networkingv1.NetworkPolicyEgressRule `json:"egress,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,3,rep,name=egress"`
270	}
271	
272	func init() {
273		SchemeBuilder.Register(&CertManager{}, &CertManagerList{})
274	}

text: Now let me look at the TrustManager type for the correct marker syntax.

content:
1	package v1alpha1
2	
3	import (
4		corev1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
5		metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
6	)
7	
8	func init() {
9		SchemeBuilder.Register(&TrustManager{}, &TrustManagerList{})
10	}
11	
12	// TrustManagerList contains a list of TrustManager resources.
13	//
14	// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object
15	// +kubebuilder:object:root=true
16	type TrustManagerList struct {
17		metav1.TypeMeta `json:",inline"`
18	
19		// metadata is the standard list's metadata.
20		// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata
21		metav1.ListMeta `json:"metadata"`
22		Items           []TrustManager `json:"items"`
23	}
24	
25	// TrustManager describes the configuration and information about the managed trust-manager deployment.
26	// The name must be `cluster` to make TrustManager a singleton, allowing only one instance per cluster.
27	// When a TrustManager CR is created, trust-manager operand is deployed in the cert-manager namespace.
28	//
29	// +genclient
30	// +genclient:nonNamespaced
31	// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object
32	// +kubebuilder:object:root=true
33	// +kubebuilder:subresource:status
34	// +kubebuilder:resource:path=trustmanagers,scope=Cluster,categories={cert-manager-operator, trust-manager},shortName=trustmanager;tm
35	// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name="Ready",type="string",JSONPath=".status.conditions[?(@.type=='Ready')].status"
36	// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name="Message",type="string",JSONPath=".status.conditions[?(@.type=='Ready')].message"
37	// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name="AGE",type="date",JSONPath=".metadata.creationTimestamp"
38	// +kubebuilder:metadata:labels={"app.kubernetes.io/name=trustmanager", "app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager-operator"}
39	// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.metadata.name == 'cluster'",message="TrustManager is a singleton, .metadata.name must be 'cluster'"
40	// +operator-sdk:csv:customresourcedefinitions:displayName="TrustManager"
41	type TrustManager struct {
42		metav1.TypeMeta `json:",inline"`
43	
44		// metadata is the standard object's metadata.
45		// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata
46		// +required
47		metav1.ObjectMeta `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
48	
49		// spec is the specification of the desired behavior of the TrustManager.
50		// +kubebuilder:validation:Required
51		// +required
52		Spec TrustManagerSpec `json:"spec"`
53	
54		// status is the most recently observed status of the TrustManager.
55		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
56		// +optional
57		Status TrustManagerStatus `json:"status,omitempty"`
58	}
59	
60	// TrustManagerSpec defines the desired state of TrustManager.
61	// Note: trust-manager operand is always deployed in the cert-manager namespace.
62	type TrustManagerSpec struct {
63		// trustManagerConfig configures the trust-manager operand's behavior.
64		// +kubebuilder:validation:Required
65		// +required
66		TrustManagerConfig TrustManagerConfig `json:"trustManagerConfig"`
67	
68		// controllerConfig configures the operator's behavior for resource creation.
69		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
70		// +optional
71		ControllerConfig TrustManagerControllerConfig `json:"controllerConfig,omitempty"`
72	}
73	
74	// TrustManagerConfig configures the trust-manager operand's behavior.
75	type TrustManagerConfig struct {
76		// logLevel configures the verbosity of trust-manager logging.
77		// Follows [Kubernetes logging guidelines](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/devel/sig-instrumentation/logging.md#what-method-to-use).
78		// +kubebuilder:default:=1
79		// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum:=1
80		// +kubebuilder:validation:Maximum:=5
81		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
82		// +optional
83		LogLevel int32 `json:"logLevel,omitempty"`
84	
85		// logFormat specifies the output format for trust-manager logging.
86		// Supported formats are "text" and "json".
87		// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:="text";"json"
88		// +kubebuilder:default:="text"
89		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
90		// +optional
91		LogFormat string `json:"logFormat,omitempty"`
92	
93		// trustNamespace is the namespace where trust-manager looks for trust sources
94		// (ConfigMaps and Secrets containing CA certificates).
95		// Defaults to "cert-manager" if not specified.
96		// This field is immutable once set.
97		// This field can have a maximum of 63 characters.
98		// +kubebuilder:default:="cert-manager"
99		// +kubebuilder:validation:MinLength:=1
100		// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxLength:=63
101		// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="oldSelf == '' || self == oldSelf",message="trustNamespace is immutable once set"
102		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
103		// +optional
104		TrustNamespace string `json:"trustNamespace,omitempty"`
105	
106		// secretTargets configures whether trust-manager can write trust bundles to Secrets.
107		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
108		// +optional
109		SecretTargets SecretTargetsConfig `json:"secretTargets,omitempty"`
110	
111		// filterExpiredCertificates controls whether trust-manager filters out
112		// expired certificates from trust bundles before distributing them.
113		// When set to "Enabled", expired certificates are removed from bundles.
114		// When set to "Disabled", expired certificates are included (default behavior).
115		// +kubebuilder:default:="Disabled"
116		// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Enabled;Disabled
117		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
118		// +optional
119		FilterExpiredCertificates FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy `json:"filterExpiredCertificates,omitempty"`
120	
121		// defaultCAPackage configures the default CA package for trust-manager.
122		// When enabled, the operator will use OpenShift's trusted CA bundle injection mechanism.
123		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
124		// +optional
125		DefaultCAPackage DefaultCAPackageConfig `json:"defaultCAPackage,omitempty"`
126	
127		// resources defines the compute resource requirements for the trust-manager pod.
128		// ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/
129		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
130		// +optional
131		Resources corev1.ResourceRequirements `json:"resources,omitempty"`
132	
133		// affinity defines scheduling constraints for the trust-manager pod.
134		// ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/
135		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
136		// +optional
137		Affinity *corev1.Affinity `json:"affinity,omitempty"`
138	
139		// tolerations allows the trust-manager pod to be scheduled on tainted nodes.
140		// ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/taint-and-toleration/
141		// +listType=atomic
142		// +kubebuilder:validation:MinItems:=0
143		// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxItems:=50
144		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
145		// +optional
146		Tolerations []corev1.Toleration `json:"tolerations,omitempty"`
147	
148		// nodeSelector restricts which nodes the trust-manager pod can be scheduled on.
149		// ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/
150		// +mapType=atomic
151		// +kubebuilder:validation:MinProperties:=0
152		// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxProperties:=50
153		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
154		// +optional
155		NodeSelector map[string]string `json:"nodeSelector,omitempty"`
156	}
157	
158	// SecretTargetsConfig configures whether and how trust-manager can write
159	// trust bundles to Secrets.
160	//
161	// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.policy != 'Custom' || (has(self.authorizedSecrets) && size(self.authorizedSecrets) > 0)",message="authorizedSecrets must not be empty when policy is Custom"
162	// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.policy == 'Custom' || !has(self.authorizedSecrets) || size(self.authorizedSecrets) == 0",message="authorizedSecrets must be empty when policy is not Custom"
163	type SecretTargetsConfig struct {
164		// policy controls whether and how trust-manager can write trust bundles to Secrets.
165		// Allowed values are "Disabled" or "Custom".
166		// "Disabled" means trust-manager cannot write trust bundles to Secrets (default behavior).
167		// "Custom" grants trust-manager read access  to all secrets cluster-wide,
168		// and write access only to the secrets listed in authorizedSecrets.
169		// +kubebuilder:default:="Disabled"
170		// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Disabled;Custom
171		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
172		// +optional
173		Policy SecretTargetsPolicy `json:"policy,omitempty"`
174	
175		// authorizedSecrets is a list of specific secret names that trust-manager
176		// is authorized to create and update. This field is only valid when policy is "Custom".
177		// +listType=set
178		// +kubebuilder:validation:MinItems:=0
179		// +kubebuilder:validation:items:MinLength:=1
180		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
181		// +optional
182		AuthorizedSecrets []string `json:"authorizedSecrets,omitempty"`
183	}
184	
185	// DefaultCAPackageConfig configures the default CA package feature for trust-manager.
186	type DefaultCAPackageConfig struct {
187		// policy controls whether the default CA package feature is enabled.
188		// When set to "Enabled", the operator will inject OpenShift's trusted CA bundle
189		// into trust-manager, enabling the "useDefaultCAs: true" source in Bundle resources.
190		// When set to "Disabled", no default CA package is configured and Bundles cannot use useDefaultCAs (default behavior).
191		// +kubebuilder:default:="Disabled"
192		// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Enabled;Disabled
193		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
194		// +optional
195		Policy DefaultCAPackagePolicy `json:"policy,omitempty"`
196	}
197	
198	// TrustManagerControllerConfig configures the operator's behavior for
199	// creating trust-manager resources.
200	type TrustManagerControllerConfig struct {
201		// labels to apply to all resources created for the trust-manager deployment.
202		// +mapType=granular
203		// +kubebuilder:validation:MinProperties:=0
204		// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxProperties:=25
205		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
206		// +optional
207		Labels map[string]string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
208	
209		// annotations to apply to all resources created for the trust-manager deployment.
210		// +mapType=granular
211		// +kubebuilder:validation:MinProperties:=0
212		// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxProperties:=25
213		// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional
214		// +optional
215		Annotations map[string]string `json:"annotations,omitempty"`
216	}
217	
218	// FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy defines the policy for filtering expired certificates.
219	type FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy string
220	
221	const (
222		// FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicyEnabled filters out expired certificates from bundles.
223		FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicyEnabled FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy = "Enabled"
224		// FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicyDisabled includes expired certificates in bundles.
225		FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicyDisabled FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy = "Disabled"
226	)
227	
228	// SecretTargetsPolicy defines the policy for writing trust bundles to Secrets.
229	type SecretTargetsPolicy string
230	
231	const (
232		// SecretTargetsPolicyDisabled means trust-manager cannot write trust bundles to Secrets.
233		SecretTargetsPolicyDisabled SecretTargetsPolicy = "Disabled"
234		// SecretTargetsPolicyCustom grants trust-manager read access to all cluster secrets
235		// and write access to only the secrets listed in authorizedSecrets.
236		SecretTargetsPolicyCustom SecretTargetsPolicy = "Custom"
237	)
238	
239	// DefaultCAPackagePolicy defines the policy for the default CA package feature.
240	type DefaultCAPackagePolicy string
241	
242	const (
243		// DefaultCAPackagePolicyEnabled enables the default CA package feature.
244		DefaultCAPackagePolicyEnabled DefaultCAPackagePolicy = "Enabled"
245		// DefaultCAPackagePolicyDisabled disables the default CA package feature.
246		DefaultCAPackagePolicyDisabled DefaultCAPackagePolicy = "Disabled"
247	)
248	
249	// TrustManagerStatus defines the observed state of TrustManager.
250	type TrustManagerStatus struct {
251		// conditions holds information about the current state of the trust-manager deployment.
252		ConditionalStatus `json:",inline,omitempty"`
253	
254		// trustManagerImage is the container image (name:tag) used for trust-manager.
255		TrustManagerImage string `json:"trustManagerImage,omitempty"`
256	
257		// trustNamespace is the namespace where trust-manager looks for trust sources.
258		TrustNamespace string `json:"trustNamespace,omitempty"`
259	
260		// secretTargetsPolicy indicates the current secret targets policy.
261		// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Disabled;Custom
262		SecretTargetsPolicy SecretTargetsPolicy `json:"secretTargetsPolicy,omitempty"`
263	
264		// defaultCAPackagePolicy indicates the current default CA package policy.
265		// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Enabled;Disabled
266		DefaultCAPackagePolicy DefaultCAPackagePolicy `json:"defaultCAPackagePolicy,omitempty"`
267	
268		// filterExpiredCertificatesPolicy indicates the current policy for filtering expired certificates.
269		// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Enabled;Disabled
270		FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy `json:"filterExpiredCertificatesPolicy,omitempty"`
271	}
